generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T <<<<< JATROPHA. STOOL. ► Diarrha. ► Diarrha, [11] . ► Copious diarrha, with watery stools, after which he felt much relieved (second day), [18] . [260.] ► Painless diarrha, with slight cramplike jerkings in both calves, [2] . ►► *Watery diarrha, as if it spurted from him , [8] . ► Profuse mucous diarrha (after five hours and a quarter), [17] . ► *Purging (after a quarter of an hour), [29] ; (after two hours and a half), [26] . ► Severe purging, with tenesmus (after several hours), [21] . ► *Purging of a violent character (in the course of an hour), (in both cases), [10] . ► Very actively purged (after ten or fifteen minutes), [15] . ► Severely purged, [23] . ► Purged three or four times, [16] . ► Purged three or four times, very copiously (after three hours and a half), [25] . [270.] ► Stools copious, mucous, and not unlike the well-known rice-water stools, [31] . ► Bowels moved copiously upwards of a dozen times; his stools being mucous and watery (after one hour), [28] . ► Several soft stools, with nausea and much spitting of saliva, [1] . ► Three natural stools a day instead of one, [3] . ► Small stool (after four hours and one-sixth), followed by another in half an hour (51 drops), [32] . ► Stool at night, which he scarcely ever had, [5] . ► Soft stool at the usual time, followed by much rumbling (sixth day), [4a] . ► Soft stool, without relief of the pain in the abdomen, which disappeared after half an hour, with emission of much flatulence (after two hours, first day), [4a] . ► Stool softer than usual, followed by constant pain in the umbilical region (after one hour, second day), [4a] . ► *Thin stool preceded and followed by much rumbling , and at times a noise as if a bottle were being emptied (after one hour and a half), [4] . [280.] ► Stool harder than usual (second day), [4] . ► Constipation. ► After soft stools for several days, there follows constipation, with slight headache, pressing inward in the temples (sixth day), [5] . ► Stool delayed (second day), [4] . ► Stool delayed, scanty and very hard (after the proving), [4a] . URINARY ORGANS. ► Bladder. ► Every attack of nausea is accompanied by spasm of the neck of the bladder, with drawing pain and desire to urinate (tenth day), [4a] . ► Painful pressure and stitches in the neck of the bladder (tenth day), [4a] . ► Urethra. ► Clear mucus oozes slowly from the urethra, while walking and sitting, [4] . ► Stitches in the urethra (tenth day), [4a] . ► Constant irritation in the navicular fossa of the penis, like a tickling, with frequent micturition, [4] . ► Micturition. ► Desire to urinate (fourth, seventh, and tenth days), [4a] . [290.] ► Desire to urinate even after micturition (second day), [4a] . ► Much desire to urinate (second day), [4a] . ► Frequent desire to urinate, [4] ; (sixth day), [4a] . ► Very frequent desire to urinate, [4] . ► Constant desire to urinate, [4] ; (fourth, sixth, and seventh days), [4a] . ► Constant desire to urinate (though he had urinated during stool three-quarters of an hour previously), [4] . ► Renewed desire after micturition (fourth day), [4a] . ► Frequent urging to urinate (tenth day), [4a] . ► Much micturition. [4] . ► Copious discharge of quite watery urine without odor or color, [5] . [300.] ► Difficulty in making water (second day), [16] . ► Dysuria (after one hour), [24] ; (after two hours and a half), [26] ; after several hours, [21] . ► Urine. ► Remarkable increase of urine after exertion, in the evening (fourth day), [5] . ► Urine much more scanty than usual, and very dark-brown, clear, [4] . ► Urine bright yellow (second and sixth days), [4a] . ► Urine bright yellow, frothy (after one hour and a quarter, fourth day), [4a] . ► (White urine), [11] . ► (Blood with the urine), [11] . SEXUAL ORGANS. ► Aching in the genitals, as after excessive sexual intercourse, the whole day (sixth day), [5] . ► Aching of the genitals, especially of the right testicle, with diminished discharge of urine (fourth day), [5] . [310.] ► Drawing pain in the genitals, extending along the inner side of the right thigh to the knee (sixth and following days), [5] . ► Aching in the left testicle (third day), [5] . RESPIRATORY ORGANS. ► Larynx. ► Painful pressure in the right side of the larynx at night, on account of which he woke; not aggravated by touch (first night), [3] . ► Cough. ► Short, hacking, persistent cough the first day; on the second day so violent that he took golden sulphuret of antimony, [1] . ► Hollow cough (first night), [1] . ► Respiration. ► Respiration quick and panting, [10a] . ► Increased difficulty of respiration (sixth day), [4a] . CHEST. ► Pain in the region of the left third rib, above and somewhat to the inside of the nipple (after a few minutes), [6] . ► Constriction in the chest, with attacks of anxiety, so that he was unable to sleep at night, [1] . ► Violent pain in the left clavicle, forenoon (third day), [5] . [320.] ► Aching in the left clavicle, lasting only a few minutes, but returning after a few hours (second day), [5] . ► Aching, amounting to a throbbing pain in the left clavicle, in the morning (third day), [5] . ► Pressure in the left pectoral muscles, which seems to make respiration difficult (seventh day), [4a] . ► Painful pressure in the right pectoral muscles (fourth day), [4a] . ► Pressive pain in the right pectoral muscles (tenth day), [4a] . ► Frequent stitches behind the cartilages of the sixth and seventh ribs (seventh day), [4a] . ► Sudden violent stitches to the left of the ensiform cartilage, behind the costal cartilages, which arrest the breathing (sixth day), [4a] . ► Front. ► Tension in the middle of the sternum, [6] . ► Pressure deep in the chest behind the sternum (second day), [4a] . ► Sticking pain, extending from above downward deep in the chest, behind the sternum, [4] . HEART AND PULSE. ► Præcordium. [330.] ► Feeling of sinking and nausea in the præcordial region, followed by soft stool (second day), [5] . ► Heart's Action. ► The beating of the heart suddenly became distinct to the hand, and was even felt in the chest, [4] . ► Violent beating of the heart (after one hour), [5] . ► Violent beating of the heart, for several days, [5] . ► Violent beating of the heart, shaking the chest, when walking slowly about the room (tenth day), [4a] . ► Palpitation (after an hour and a half), [22] . ► Palpitation of the heart, after moving about (after six hours), [5] . ► Uneasiness at night from palpitation, and rush of thoughts, [5] . ► Heart's action very irregular, and so weak that the impulse against the walls of the chest could with great difficulty be perceived, [10a] . ► Pulse. ► Pulse accelerated, for two days, [5] . [340.] ► Frequent and weak pulse (after several hours), [16] . ► Pulse slow, full, and soft (after one hour, tenth day), [4a] . ► Pulse diminished 2 beats (after one hour), [4] . ► Pulse 5 beats less than usual (after a quarter of an hour, fourth day), [4a] . ► Pulse 6 or 7 beats slower than usual (after half an hour); 10 beats slower than usual (after one hour, tenth day), [4a] . ► Pulse 9 beats less than usual (after three-quarters of an hour); 5 beats less than usual (after one hour and a quarter, fourth day), [4a] . ► Pulse 140 and weak (after four hours), [15] . ► Pulse 120 (second day), [16] . ► Pulse 87, large, full, almost jerking (after two hours, tenth day), [4a] . ► Pulse 80, strong (after one hour), [5] . [350.] ► Pulse 80, hard, and throbbing, [5] . ► Pulse irregular, of normal frequency (sixth day), [4a] . ► Pulse small, thready, and intermittent, [10a] . ► *Almost pulseless (after five hours and a quarter), [17] . NECK AND BACK. ► Nape. ► Stiffness in the nape of the neck, [4] . ► Stiffness of the muscles of the nape of the neck (tenth day), [4a] . ► Stiffness of the muscles of the nape of the neck and forehead (fourth day), [4a] . ► Back. ► Pain in the muscles across the back (seventh day), [4a] . ► Painful pressure in the lumbar, dorsal, and pectoral muscles (fourth day), [4a] . ► Transient pressure in the muscles of the back and left upper arm (first day), [4a] . [360.] ► Constant bruised feeling in the muscles of the back and chest (sixth day), [4a] . ► Dorsal. ► Pain in the region of the sixth dorsal vertebra, [6] . ► Constant pain in the rhomboid muscle in the region of the fourth and fifth ribs of the left side, dull pain lasting twelve hours, [6] . ► Lumbar. ► Painful stiffness of the lumbar muscles (fourth day), [4a] . ► Very transient pains in the lumbar muscles, chest, and shoulders, [4] . ► Painful thrusts from the umbilicus to the lumbar region (seventh day), [4a] . ► Feeling as from a thrust in the muscles, in the region of the small of the back, while walking, [4] . ► Stitches in the lumbar muscles (seventh day), [4a] . ► Feeling of soreness in the small of the back (after one hour), [7] . ► A sore feeling in the small of the back and rectum as in diarrha (after one hour), [7] . [370.] ► Crawling beneath the skin of the small of the back (after three hours), [4] . EXTREMITIES IN GENERAL. ► Muscles of the extremities contracted by violent spasms, [10a] . ► Painful feeling of weakness in the limbs, especially in the knees (fourth day), [4a] . ► Cramps in the hands and feet (second day), [18] . ► Most severe cramps in the arms and legs (after one hour), [28] . ► Drawing pains in various parts of the extremities, in part seeming to be under the skin, [4] . ► Pressure in the left calf, right knee, and right wrist (tenth day), [4a] . ► Pressure in the upper arms and right calf alternately, though only while sitting (second day), [4a] . ► Pressive pain on the back of the right foot, on the posterior surface of the left upper arm, and in the flexor surface of the left forearm (sixth day), [4a] . ► Pressive pain in the muscles of the shoulder and back, in the knees and calves, with a feeling of weakness (sixth day), [4a] . [380.] ► Pressive and contractive pain in various parts of the extremities, especially in the wrists, toes, and calves, [4] . SUPERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Bruised feeling in the muscles of the arm (first day), [4a] . ► Shoulder. ► Pain in the right shoulder and arm, extending into the hand, worse along the ulnar nerve, [5] . ► Rheumatic pain in the right and afterwards in the left shoulder (third day), [5] . ► Drawing in the muscles of the left shoulder (fourth day), [4a] . ► Pressure in the shoulder-joint (fourth day), [4a] . ► Pressure in the left shoulder (tenth day), [4a] . ► Bruised pain in the shoulder-joints (first day), [4a] . ► Bruised and stiff feeling in the shoulders (second day), [4a] . ► Arm. ► Weariness in the upper arms, [4] . [390.] ► Pressure in the muscles of the right upper arms (tenth day), [4a] . ► Forearm. ► Aching along the left ulna nerve (after one and two hours), [5] . ► Gnawing in the ulnar nerve, [5] . ► Pain along the ulnar nerves, extending from the left elbow to the tips of the fourth and fifth fingers (immediately), [5] . ► Pain, with a feeling of numbness extending from the elbows to the tips of the fourth and fifth fingers, with a burning, causing insensibility, lasting half an hour (after half an hour, and repeated after four days), [5] . ► Internal pressure in the flexor muscles of the left forearm (fourth day), [4a] . ► Hand. ► Drawing on the back of the left hand (seventh day), [4a] . ► Pain in the metacarpus of the right thumb, extending to the wrist and hand (after five hours), [5] . ► Fingers. ► Violent pain in the first joint of the right index finger, extending to the tip (after five hours), [5] . ► Aching in the first joint of the right thumb, extending to the wrist, in the morning (second day), [5] . INFERIOR EXTREMITIES. [400.] ► Gait tottering; the body involuntarily inclined towards the left, while walking (fourth day), [4a] . ► Feeling of stiffness and unsteadiness in the legs, when walking (fourth day), [4a] . ► Knee. ► Pains in the hollows of both knees, for several days, [5] . ► Pressive pain in the right knee and left calf, on walking slowly up and down the room (seventh day), [4a] . ► Painful tearing in the left knee and malleolus (after seven hours), [5] . ► Bruised pain in the knees, [4] . ► Bruised feeling in the knees, while sitting, [4] . ► Leg. ► Stiffness of both calves (fourth day), [4a] . ► Feeling of stiffness in the left calf (second day), [4a] . ► Feeling of stiffness in the calves, while walking up and down the room (seventh day), [4a] . [410.] ► Feeling of stiffness and pressure in the left calf and back of the left foot (first day), [4a] . ► Pain in the calves, even when walking, with internal drawing in them (fourth day), [4a] . ► Constant compression in the muscles of the left leg, especially in the lower portion of the calf (tenth day), [4a] . ► Internal jerking and pressure in the left calf and toes, more towards the inner margin of the tibia, extending up and down the inner surface (fourth day), [4a] . ► *Slight cramplike jerkings in both calves, with painless diarrha , [2] . ► *Violent cramplike pain in the lower legs, and cramps in the calves , which become knotted , [8] . ► Slight cramps (after a quarter of an hour), [29] ; (after three hours and a half), [25] . ► Severe cramps and pains in the legs and feet (after three hours and three-quarters), [17] . ► Violent cramps, in the evening (after several hours), [21] . ► Violent cramps and twitchings, "the calves were twisted around to the shins." (Hering.) [420.] ► Pressure and stiffness in the left calf, especially towards the tibia (sixth day), [4a] . ► Painful pressure in the external muscles ot the legs, with internal crawling (fourth day), [4a] . ► Pressing in the left calf (seventh day), [4a] . ► Pressive pain in the calves, [4] . ► Constant pressive-drawing pain in the outer portion of the right calf (first day), [4a] . ► Sticking in the calves, [4] . ► Constant bruised feeling in the calves, especially while sitting (sixth day), [4a] . ► Peculiar bruised feeling, extending from the knees into the legs and becoming seated in the ankles (seventh day), [4a] . ► Scraping feeling on the inner surface of the left tibia (seventh day), [4a] . ► Pulsation in the left calf (fourth day), [4a] . ► Ankles. [430.] ► Rheumatic pain in the left ankle, worse in the forenoon (third day), [5] . ► Violent rheumatic pain in the left ankle (third day), [5] . ► Pressive pain in the right ankle (tenth day), [4a] . ► Pressing pain in the left ankle, left upper arm, left hypochondrium, and left inguinal region (seventh day), [4a] . ► Foot. ► Rheumatic pain in the ball of the right foot, in the morning (fourth day), [5] . ► Slight drawings in the balls of both feet (first day), [3] . ► Rheumatic drawing in the sole of the left foot, extending into the ball of the great toe, [3] . ► Drawing on the back of the right foot (tenth day), [4a] . ► Sticking and pressure in the sole of the left foot and in the left calf (second day), [4a] . ► Sticking and jerking in the sole of the left foot uninterruptedly, while sitting, [4a] . [440.] ► The heels are very sensitive when stepping upon them (fourth day), [4a] . ► Toes. ► Pressure and drawing in the great toe and on the back of the left foot (fourth day), [4a] . ► Stitches in the left great toe (sixth day), [4a] . ► Inward twitching in the toes of the right foot (fourth day), [4a] . ► Tearing in the toes and on the back of the left foot in rapid alternations (tenth day), [4a] . ► Crawling in the toes (tenth day), [4a] . ► Constant crawling in the left great toe (seventh day), [4a] . GENERAL SYMPTOMS. ► Objective. ► Depression very great, in some cases, [31] . ► Great depression of the vascular system (after one hour and a half), [22] . ► General weakness, [11] . [450.] ► Extreme weariness and sleepiness (first day), [3] . ► Weakness (tenth day), [4a] . ► Great weakness, and heaviness of the head (tenth day), [4a] . ► So exceedingly weak as hardly to be able to walk about, after the perspiration broke out (after about one hour and a half); this debility greatly increased during the next half hour; in another half hour he had so far recovered as to be able to walk to the London Hospital; was very weak (after four hours), [15] . ► On the slightest exertion, painful weakness of the muscles used, even when writing or standing (fourth day), [4a] . ► Prostration (after half an hour), [7] . ► Great prostration, with frequent and weak pulse, in the evening (after several hours), [16] . ► Great restlessness, [10a] . ► Subjective. ► More susceptible than usual to wine, [1] . ► Feeling of agreeable warmth, ethereal lightness, [2] . SKIN. ► Objective. [460.] ► Several small pimples on the wrist and back of the right hand (second day), disappearing after a few days), [5] . ► Two badly looking, hard though not painful pimples on the middle of the right cheek, slowly disappearing, [5] . ► Sore painful pimples on the back of the neck (second day), [5] . ► Sore painful pimples in the bend of the right thigh (second day), [5] . ► Ulceration, smarting, and tension in the right corner of the mouth, in the morning (fourth day), [5] . ► Subjective. ► Prickling on the back of the left hand (second day), [4a] . ► Crawling in the skin of both lower legs and feet (tenth day), [4a] . ► Itching and tension of the lips, in the morning (second day), [5] . ► Itching and slight tension around the mouth, better after rubbing (after two hours), [5] . ► Itching between the right index and middle fingers, and on the palm of the hand (after seven hours), [5] . [470.] ► Itching between the toes in bed (first night), [5] . ► Violent itching, as from the itch, between the thumb and index finger, at first aggravated then relieved by rubbing, [5] . ► Violent itching of the commissure between the first and second fingers, relieved by rubbing, in the morning (third day), [5] . ► Internal itching of the fingers, worse on the left hand (fourth day), [4a] . SLEEP AND DREAMS. ► Sleepiness. ► Much yawning, with nausea, accumulation of saliva and many eructations (tenth day), [4a] . ► Drowsiness (after a quarter of an hour), [29] ; (after one hour), [24] . ► Drowsiness very marked, many of the patients falling asleep in the cabs on their way to the hospital, and many dropping off to sleep repeatedly in the waiting-room, but they could all be immediately aroused, [31] . ► Drowsy, [17] ; (second day), [16] . ► Very drowsy, [27] . ► Very drowsy and prostrated (after one hour), [28] . [480.] ► Very drowsy, hot and feverish, after vomiting (after one hour), [20] . ► He thought that he had a fever so that he had to sleep, without being cold or hot or sweaty (first day), [9] . ► Dropped off to sleep every half minute, although easily aroused (after five hours and a quarter), [17] . ► He is scarcely able to wake from sleep; in the morning and afterwards; he is obliged to forcibly make himself move, after which he feels better, [1] . ► Sleeplessness. ► Sleepless, with throbbing of the pulse for several hours after lying down (third night), [5] . ► Restlessness at night on account of rush of thought and palpitation, [5] . ► Restless tossing about, sleepless, with throbbing of the pulse, the whole night, [5] . ► Attacks of anxiety at night did not permit sleep till towards morning, [1] . ► On account of angry conversation which he had had in the evening, he awoke at 2 A.M., because he had it in his mind in sleep; he fell asleep again and dreamed of it, [3] . ► Dreams. ► Incessant dreams, [1] . FEVER. ► Chilliness. [490.] ► Skin cold and moist, [10a] . ► Chilliness with a feeling of coldness extending through the abdomen; the palms of the hands previously dry become moist and cold (fourth day), [4a] . ► Much chilliness, cold hands and blue nails (after three hours, first day), [4a] . ► Much chilliness, with cold tips of the fingers (tenth day), [4a] . ► *Coldness of the body , shivering and clammy sweat, with vomiting and diarrha, [8] . ► Cold shivering over the whole body (after one hour), [7] . ► Chilliness up and down the back (fourth day), [4a] . ► Chilliness creeping over the back, [4] . ► Chilliness through the back extending into the upper arms, constant, even in a warm room, [4] . ► Chilliness in the spinal marrow (second day), [4a] . [500.] ► Chilliness starting from the lumbar vertebræ (seventh day), [4a] . ► Chilliness deep in the lumbar vertebræ, with desire for stool two or three hours earlier than usual in the morning, [4] . ► Much chilliness in the region of the lumbar vertebræ (tenth day), [4a] . ► Constant chilliness in the back (sixth day), [4a] . ► Great chilliness creeping through all the limbs, extending to the scalp, [4] . ► At times coldness through the back (second day), [4a] . ► Much coldness in the back, extending into the upper arm (first day), [4a] . ► Great coldness deep in the spine, [4] . ► Extremities cold, nails at times blue, [4] . ► The hands hitherto warm became cold and the nails blue (after half an hour), [4] . [510.] ► Hands rather cold (after four hours), [15] . ► Shivering in the back (sixth day), [4a] . ► Frequent shivering through the region of the lumbar vertebræ, [4] . ► Required application of warmth to the extremities, in the evening (after several hours), [16] . ► Heat. ► Hot and feverish (after one hour), [15] . ► Hot and feverish during the evening, [30] . ► Very hot and feverish (second day), [18] . ► Slight fever, as indicated by rapid pulse, hot skin and dry tongue (second day), [21] . ► Very feverish (second day); slightly so (third day), [16] . ► Feeling of heat extending from the stomach to the spine, repeated in rapid succession (fourth day), [4a] . [520.] ► Heat across the back (fourth day), [4a] . ► Sweat. ► Promotes perspiration, [11] . ► Profuse perspiration broke out upon the whole surface of the body (after about one hour and a half), [15] . ► Sweat during sleep, [1] . CONDITIONS. ► Aggravation. ► ( Morning ), Desire for stool; aching in thumb; pain in ball of foot. ► ( Forenoon ), Pain in ankle. ► ( Evening ), Thirst. ► ( Night ), Attacks of anxiety; feeling in mouth; in bed, itching in anus; pressure in anus; in bed, itching between toes. ► ( On entering house ), Pain in temple returns . ► ( Pressure ), Pain in stomach; pain in umbilical region. ► ( Rubbing ), Itching between thumb and finger. ► ( While sitting ), Muscular symptoms; bruised feeling in calves. ► ( Sitting down after walking about room ), General bruised sensation. ► ( On stooping ), Feeling of cramp in stomach. ► ( While walking ), Feeling in small of back. ► ( In warm room ), Rumbling in abdomen returned. ► Amelioration. ► ( Open air ), Symptoms in general; pain in temples; rumbling in abdomen. SUPPLEMENT: JATROPHA. Authorities. 33 , Dr. C. Hering, North Amer. Journ. of Hom., vol. iv, 1851, p. 447; 34 , E. J. Waring, Pharm. Journ., 2d Ser., vol. vii, p. 554, a young Hindoo swallowed three or four fresh seeds of "Curcas multifidus;" 35 , Dr. Marrett, Madras Med. Journ., 1861, p. 37 (Pharm. Journ., 2d Ser., vol. vii, p. 553), a person swallowed fifteen to twenty seeds. ► Within an hour and a half after ingestion, burning sensations in the throat and stomach commenced, followed by profuse purging, vomiting, violent cramps of the extremities, which subsequently extended to the muscles of the abdomen and back, and occasional twitchings of those of the back, almost like those observed in tetanus. This was followed by deafness, and although the patient seemed all the time to be quite sensible, he failed on recovery, which occurred after two days of intense suffering, to recall any of the events of his illness subsequent to the occurrence of the purging, which was one of the earliest symptoms, [35] . ► Violent purging and vomiting, intense heat and pain in the stomach, with great prostration of the vital powers, were the principal symptoms, [34] . ► In Cuba, it is said that placing the hands in cold water will cause the effects to cease, [33] . Clinical: Blepharospasm; Borborygmi Cholera; Cholerine; Cramps; Diarrhœa; Eyelids, margins of, inflamed; Heels, sensitive; Mouth, ulceration of; Skin, affections of; Vomiting; Worms

Painful pressure r. side of larynx, at night, waking him, not by touch. Short, hacking, persistent cough. Hollow cough. Respiration quick, panting. Respiration difficult. Muscles of limbs contracted by violent spasms. Pains in limbs and joints. Cramps in muscles of (upper) arms. Cramps in calves. (Cholera.). Cramp-like pains in the legs, with cramps and knotting of the calves. Violent cramps in legs and feet. Tingling in the toes. Itching between the toes at night. The heels are very sensitive when walking on them. Convulsions. General coldness of the body. Extreme weariness and sleepiness. Prostration, with frequent and weak pulse; by slightest exertion. More susceptible than usual to wine.

Summer : severe diarrhœa occurring suddenly. Left : quivering in upper eyelid ; noise as of liquids in abdomen on side. Mouth feels as if scalded ; as if balls were rolling together in abdomen ; rumbling as if a bottle was being emptied. Pain : in region of navel. Severe pain : in abdomen. Lancinating stinging pain : in abdomen. Violent cramplike pain : in lower legs. Stitches : in anus and rectum. Griping, sticking pains : in abdomen. Violent cramps : in lower limbs. Cramps : in calves ; in abdomen ; in bronchial muscles. Violent pressing pain : in temples. Contracting pains : in stomach. Constrictive pains : in epigastrium, in gastric region. Slight cramplike jerkings : in both calves. Spasmodic constriction : in throat. Persistent dull pressure : in stomach. Smarting : of margin of lids. Burning : in stomach ; of tongue ; in mouth and throat ; in abdomen. Burning hot ears. Heat : of head ; in back part of head ; of mouth ; in face ; in ears. Dryness : of mouth ; of tongue ; in fauces ; in throat. Numbness : of tongue. Stiffness : of muscles on forehead and neck. Sinking : in pit of stomach. Heaviness : of head. Tingling : in toes. Quivering : in left upper eyelid. Itching : of margins of lids ; of nose ; between toes at night. TOUCH. PASSIVE MOTION. INJURIES. [45] [Jatr.]. Touch : abdomen sore ; heels sore when walking on them. Woman, æt. 22 ; cholera. Woman, æt. 31 ; cholera. Man, æt. 40 ; cholera.

keynotes.text: Jatropha is one of the most active of the Euphorbians and strongly resembles Croton in its action, especially in the urgent gushing stools and the irritation and pimples of the skin. One of the most notable characteristics of Jatropha is the very marked and peculiar gurgling it causes: "Much noise in abdomen as if a bottle was being emptied, followed later by a thin stool." "Sudden desire for stool, and constant noises as of liquids in abdomen, especially on left side." The diarrhœa is very often associated with coldness; with mottled blue surface; with cold sweat. At times the purging is accompanied by copious (and easy) vomiting of watery albuminous ropy liquid. Sudden desire for stool is marked. Water is almost immediately vomited. It corresponds more to the earlier than the collapse stage of cholera. Cramps are predominant; griping cutting pains; sensation as if balls were rolling together in abdomen. The patient writhes about in bed with pain. Kent (H. P., vi. 355) describes the sphere of Jatr. in cholera thus: "The characteristic is a rice-water discharge; it has vomiting and purging; more or less sweat; more or less cramps; suppression of urine, the great prostration, syncope and rapid onset peculiar to cholera; and it has this to differentiate it from all other remedies─the vomit and purging and evacuations are thick, albuminous, lumpy, instead of thin and watery." Walking in open air rumbling; = pain in heels; > headache. Open air > headache; = rumbling. In room, headache . Patient seeks to cool himself by throwing off covers and lying on ground. Touch and pressure . At night: mouth dry; itching between toes. After midnight: profuse watery diarrhœa. In morning: stools headache; metallic, bloody taste; much spitting of saliva. Summer diarrhœa, occurring suddenly.

_raw: Antidote to its effects: placing hands in cold water. Compare: Euphorbiaceæ, especially Croton, Euphorb. coroll. In cholera, Verat. (Verat. has more pain and more pronounced collapse; it has not the albuminous vomiting of Jatr. nor so much rumbling). Ars. (vomits water as soon as drunk). Elat. (gushing, noisy stools). Jatropha urens.
Effects cease by placing hands in cold water
Compare : the Euphorbiacea, viz. : Crot. tig., Euphorb. coral., Mancinella ; also Veratr. in cholera

mental_emotional.text: Depression. Great anxiety. Anxiety with burning pain in stomach and coldness of the body. Attacks of anxiety at night constricting chest and preventing sleep till morning. Quietude of mind; indifference to pain. Great general prostration of strength. Ecstasy, feeling of lively warmth, etherial lightness during the painful diarrhœa.

Apathy ; indifference to pain. Anxiety and anguish. Anxiety, with burning in stomach and coldness of body. Giddiness, followed by unconsciousness and delirium. Head hot, stupefaction, with yawning and nausea.

head.text: Giddiness, followed by unconsciousness and delirium. Heat and heaviness of the head. Head hot; stupefaction, with yawning and nausea. Heat in head, face, and ears. Headache, with nausea and vomiturition beginning in the morning. Violent pressing pain in the temples, ceasing in open air and reappearing when entering a room. Stiffness of muscles on forehead and neck.

Violent pressing pain in temples, > in open air, and reappearing when entering room. Headache, with nausea and vomiturition, beginning in morning. Head and heaviness of head. Stiffness of muscles on forehead and neck.

eyes.text: Itching and smarting of margins of lids. Twitching of l. upper lid. Bright and dark spots before eyes. Black points before eyes.
Quivering in left upper lid. Itching and smarting of margins of lids.

ears.text: Burning hot ears, with heat in the back part of the head.
Burning hot ears, with heat in back part of head.

nose.text: Itching of the nose while eating. Ulcers in the nose (and mouth).
Itching of nose while eating. Ulcers in nose and mouth.

face.text: Hot face and head; chilliness in the back. Pate face with blue margins round eyes. Painful cracked lips.
Pale face with blue margins around eyes. Painful cracked lips.

mouth_throat.text: Metallic, bloody taste, with much spitting of saliva (in the morning). Increased accumulation of thin saliva. Long-continued pain and burning of the tongue. Numbness of tongue, with heat and dryness of mouth. Dryness of mouth and tongue, without thirst (at night); mouth feels as if scalded. Dryness in fauces and throat. Burning in the mouth and throat, followed by dryness. Spasmodic constriction in throat, ascending from stomach.

Metallic, bloody taste, with much spitting of saliva (in morning). Long continued pain and burning of tongue. Numbness of tongue, with heat and dryness of mouth. Increased accumulation of thin saliva. Burning in mouth and throat, followed by dryness. Dryness of mouth and tongue, without thirst (at night) ; mouth feels as if scalded. Ulcers in nose and mouth. Dryness in fauces and throat. Spasmodic constriction in throat, ascending from stomach.

stomach_abdomen.text: Violent, unquenchable thirst, not satisfied by drinking water. Dreads to drink on account of nausea. Eructations of air. Vomiting of a large mass of dark-green bile. Glass of water almost immediately vomited. Easy vomiting of large quantities of watery, albuminous substances; at same time watery diarrhœa, with spasmodically contracting pains in the stomach, burning in the stomach, cramps in the calves; coldness of body; viscid sweat (cholerine). Vomiting of pregnant women. Heat and burning in the stomach. Inflammation of the stomach and of the intestines. Sensation of sinking, with nausea in the pit of the stomach. Persistent dull pressure in stomach. Pain deep in abdomen, behind the navel. Abdomen swollen, and sore to the touch. Sensation as if balls were rolling together in abdomen. Lancinating, stinging pain with the colic. Burning in abdomen; seeks to cool himself by throwing off covering and lying on ground. Painful thrusts from umbilicus to lumbar region. Tympanites. Rumbling in the abdomen with colic; > when walking in the open air. Noise like the gurgling of water coming out of the bung-hole of a barrel during the fecal discharge. Noise in the intestines, as if a bottle were emptied, or as if a fluid were running in the intestines; not > by a loose stool.

APPETITE, THIRST. DESIRES, AVERSIONS. [14] [Jatr.]. Inextinguishable thirst, not satisfied by drinking cold water. θ Cholera. Dreads to drink on account of nausea. Eructations. Hiccough, followed by copious vomiting of bile. θ Cholera. Vomiting : of large masses of dark green bile and mucus ; of large quantities of watery, albuminous substances ; water is almost immediately vomited. Very easy vomiting of a great quantity of watery, glairy fluid, accompanied by watery diarrhœa ; spasmodically contracting pains in stomach, burning in stomach, cramps in calves and coldness of body. θ Cholera. Uninterrupted vomiting. θ Cholera. Sensation of sinking, with nausea in pit of stomach. Crampy, constrictive pains in epigastrium. Persistent dull pressure in stomach. Heat and burning in stomach. Spasmodically contracting pains in stomach. Much noise in abdomen, as if a full bottle was emptied, followed later by thin stool. Rumbling in abdomen, with colic ; > when walking in open air. Abdomen : distended and soft ; very much inflated ; distended, tympanitic ; swollen and sore to touch ; contracted. θ Cholera. Pain in region of navel, deep. Burning in abdomen ; seeks to cool himself by throwing off covering and lying on ground. Lancinating, stinging pain with colic.

stool_rectum.text: Sudden desire for stool, and constant noises as of liquids in abdomen, esp. l. side. Watery diarrhœa; it gushes from him like a torrent. First stage of cholera, before collapse. Very copious mushy stools with quantities of lumbrici and threadworms. Stool watery and in gushes. Stools like rice-water. Profuse watery diarrhœa, from a cold, weakening patient greatly. Constipation. Stitches in anus and rectum.

Sudden desire for stool, and constant noises as of liquids in abdomen, especially in left side. Thin stool, preceded and followed by much rumbling, and at times a noise as if a bottle was being emptied. Colic followed by discharge of large, watery, odorless stool. Continued discharge of water by stool. Stools painless, thin, watery, with loud, fluid rumbling, and gushing out of stool ; < mornings. Watery diarrhœa, as if it spurted from him. Diarrhœa profuse ; gushing out like a torrent. Severe diarrhœa occurring suddenly in Summer, in connection with a sharp attack of vomiting. After taking cold, sleepless ; slight pain in bowels ; after midnight profuse watery discharge ; a few hours later, again and again, weakening patient greatly ; stools profuse, watery, without smell. θ Diarrhœa. Easy vomiting of large quantities of watery substance like albumen ; diarrhœa, contents of rectum gush out like a torrent ; anxiety, with burning at stomach ; anguish, with coldness of body ; viscid sweat ; violent cramps in lower limbs ; calves look like flat splints ; abdomen flattened after many stools. θ Cholerine. Vomiting, with cramp in abdomen ; frequent, profuse, pale, watery, urgent stools ; loud rumbling, as of water pouring from a bottle ; abdomen flat ; face pale, shrivelled ; great thirst for water or lemonade. During menstruation, which had ceased for thirty hours, uninterrupted vomiting of scentless, white, gelatinous fluid ; cramps in calves and brachial muscles ; body blue, cold as marble, and covered with cold, clammy sweat ; pulse imperceptible ; abdomen contracted ; unquenchable thirst ; drinks much cold water ; burning in abdomen, which she seeks to relieve by lying on ground ; mind placid, rather ecstatic, taking little notice of her cramps and other affections. θ Cholera morbus. Fearful vomiting, coming up at once in quantity, of watery or glairy fluid, with crampy, constrictive pains in gastric region, or burning in stomach ; continued discharge of water from anus ; cramps in calves ; general coldness of body. θ Cholera. Sudden, violent vomiting of watery fluids, with cramps in stomach ; cool skin ; weak, soft, slow pulse ; great debility ; anxiety and fear, as though cramps in calves would set in ; stool diarrheic yesterday ; to-day neither stool nor urine. θ Cholera. First stage of cholera, before period of collapse. Constipation. Very abundant alvine evacuations, mushy, with a large number of lumbrici and ascarides. Griping, sticking pains in abdomen, continuing a long time, accompanied by sensation as if balls were rolling together in abdomen ; constipation ; pains occasionally so severe that he shrieked and writhed about in bed ; after five drops a copious evacuation with a quantity of lumbrici and ascarides ; the same with the following evacuations. Stitches in anus and rectum.

urinary.text: Frequent desire to urinate; urine, pale yellow, frothy.
Frequent desire to urinate ; urine pale yellow, frothy.

male.text: Aching in genitals as after excessive sexual intercourse. Drawing in genitals, extending along inner side r. thigh to navel.

female.text: PREGNANCY. PARTURITION. LACTATION. [24] [Jatr.]. Vomiting during pregnancy.

chest_heart.text: Pain in region of l. 3rd rib inside nipple. Constriction in chest with anxiety, preventing sleep. Violent (aching) pain in l. clavicle. Pressure in l. pectoral muscles; later in r. Frequent, sudden, violent stitches to l. of ensiform cartilage, behind the costal cartilages, which arrest the breathing. Frequent stitches behind the cartilages of the 6th and 7th ribs. Sticking, from above downwards, deep in chest behind sternum. Feeling of sinking and nausea in the precordial region, followed by soft stool. Violent palpitation, shaking the chest, when moving slowly about the room. Pulse: irregular; small, thready, intermittent; greatly slowed. Almost pulseless.

back.text: Stiffness of muscles of nape of neck; and forehead. Bruised feeling in muscles of back and chest. Stiffness in lumbar region.

skin.text: Pimples: on wrist; back of hand; cheek; back of neck bend of r. thigh. Itching and slight tension round mouth. Itching: between fingers; in palm; between toes in bed at night; first , then > by rubbing.

sleep.text: Excessive drowsiness. Restlessness at night on account of rush of thought and palpitation. Attacks of anxiety at night did not permit sleep.

fever_chill.text: Chilliness in the back, with heat in the face and head. Chilliness, with cold hands and blue nails. Coldness of the whole body; mottled blue; chilliness and clammy perspiration. Cold hands, with heat in the mouth and throat. Heat in head, face, and ears. General cold, clammy perspiration. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main.

Chilliness in back, with heat in face and head. Chilliness with cold hands and blue nails. Coldness of body. Body cold as marble and mottled blue. θ Cholera. Cold hands, with heat in mouth and throat. Heat in head, face and ears. General cold, clammy perspiration. Cold, clammy sweat on skin. θ Cholera.

extremities.text: Cramps in brachial muscles. θ Cholera. Slight cramplike jerkings in both calves, with painless diarrhœa. Cramps in calves. θ Cholera. Violent, cramplike pain in lower legs, and cramps in calves, which become knotted. Violent cramps in legs and feet. Tingling in toes. Itching between toes at night. The heels are very sensitive when walking on them.

modalities.text: REST. POSITION. MOTION. [35] [Jatr.]. Walking : in open air, rumbling in abdomen ; on heels, sensitive. Writhed about in bed with pain. Morning : headache begins ; metallic, bloody taste, with much spitting of saliva ; stools <. Night : dryness of mouth and tongue, without thirst ; itching between toes. After midnight : profuse watery discharge ; a few hours later again and again. Open air : pressing pain in temples > ; rumbling in abdomen. Entering room : pressing pain in temples returns. Seeks to cool himself by throwing off covering and lying on ground.
